# US-007: View today's tasks ordered by priority with rationale - Implementation Plan

**Generated by:** Archetipo Planning Team
**Date:** 2026-05-06

---

## User Story

**Epic:** EP-002 - Developer Daily View
**Priority:** HIGH | **Story Points:** 3

**Story**
As a developer, I want to see my tasks for today ordered by priority with the rationale notes from Valentina, so that I know what to work on and why it matters before I commit.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] The page lists only the tasks assigned to this dev for the active sprint
- [ ] Tasks are displayed in priority order as set in the management UI
- [ ] Each task shows the rationale note (if present) directly under the task title
- [ ] When no tasks are assigned, an empty-state message is shown instead of an empty list

---

## Technical Solution

We fill the content slot of the public dev page `/dev/[token]` (the shell from US-006) with a server-rendered list of the dev's tasks for the currently active sprint, ordered by `position`. The active sprint is the single sprint of the profile owner whose `[startDate, endDate]` window contains "today" - this matches the [DECISION] in the backlog that activation derives from dates with no manual flag. Rationale (added by US-005) is rendered as a muted secondary line below the title when present, mirroring the management view in `TaskRow` so devs see the same artefact Valentina sees. The empty state is a deliberate component, not just a missing list. This story has hard dependencies on US-006 (the `/dev/[token]` route and `findActiveProfileByToken`) and US-005 (the `rationale` field on `Task`); both must be merged first.

- **Active-sprint helper**: add `getActiveSprintForOwner(ownerId, now)` to `src/lib/sprint.ts`, building on the existing `isActiveSprint`. Returns the single `Sprint` whose date window contains `now` for that owner, or `null`. Implemented as a Prisma query `findFirst` with `startDate <= now AND endDate >= now`. No schema change needed - `Sprint` already has `ownerId` and the `[ownerId, startDate]` index.
- **Today's tasks helper**: add `getTodaysTasksForDev({ devProfileId, sprintId })` to a new `src/lib/task.ts`. Single `prisma.task.findMany` filtered by `assignedDevId` and `sprintId`, ordered `[{ position: "asc" }, { createdAt: "asc" }]` to mirror the admin backlog ordering exactly. Returns the slim shape needed by the UI (`id`, `title`, `description`, `rationale`, `position`).
- **Page wiring** (`src/app/dev/[token]/page.tsx`): after the existing `findActiveProfileByToken`, call `getActiveSprintForOwner(profile.ownerId, new Date())`. If null OR if `getTodaysTasksForDev` returns `[]`, render `<TodayEmptyState />`; otherwise render `<TodayTaskList tasks={...} />`. Both replace the `data-slot="today"` placeholder reserved by US-006.
- **`TodayTaskList`** in `src/app/dev/_components/TodayTaskList.tsx`: server component, maps tasks to `TodayTaskCard`. No client interactivity in this story - selection/commitment is US-008.
- **`TodayTaskCard`** in `src/app/dev/_components/TodayTaskCard.tsx`: title (heading), optional rationale rendered below as a muted secondary line (no `line-clamp` here - dev needs full reasoning, unlike the dense admin row). Description is also shown when present, visually distinct from rationale (rationale = "why", description = "what").
- **`TodayEmptyState`** in `src/app/dev/_components/TodayEmptyState.tsx`: neutral message ("No tasks for today yet - check back later or reach out to your PO"). Same component for both "no active sprint" and "active sprint with zero tasks for this dev" - the dev does not need to distinguish, and the message is identical from their perspective.
- **No new dependencies, no schema changes, no middleware change** (the `/dev/*` exclusion is already handled by US-006).
- **Visual consistency**: reuse design tokens from `globals.css` and the dev shell styles introduced by US-006. Mockups in `docs/mockups/US-007/` are the visual reference.

---

## Test Strategy

We verify each AC at the appropriate level: data-shape and ordering at integration level (where the Prisma query lives), UX states end-to-end. The cross-dev isolation guarantee ("only tasks assigned to this dev") is covered explicitly because it is the only data leakage risk in this story.

- **Unit** - `getActiveSprintForOwner`: returns the sprint when `now` falls inside `[startDate, endDate]`, returns `null` for upcoming/past sprints, returns `null` for sprints owned by a different user.
- **Integration** - `getTodaysTasksForDev`: returns only tasks where `assignedDevId === devProfileId AND sprintId === sprintId`; respects `position asc, createdAt asc` ordering; returns `[]` when the dev has no tasks; never returns tasks from another dev or another sprint.
- **E2E (Playwright)** - happy path: fixture with active sprint + 3 tasks for the dev (positions 1, 2, 3) with mixed rationale; visit `/dev/{token}`, assert tasks render in the same order, assert rationale text is visible under tasks that have it, assert tasks without rationale render without an empty rationale slot.
- **E2E (Playwright)** - empty state: fixture with active sprint but no tasks for this dev; visit `/dev/{token}`, assert `TodayEmptyState` is visible and the task list is not rendered.
- **E2E (Playwright)** - no active sprint: fixture with only past/upcoming sprints; visit `/dev/{token}`, assert the same empty state is visible (single message).
- **E2E (Playwright)** - cross-dev isolation: fixture with two devs in the same sprint; visit each dev's URL and assert each sees only their own tasks.
- **E2E (Playwright)** - priority changes propagate: reorder tasks via the admin UI (or directly via DB fixture), reload `/dev/{token}`, assert the new order is reflected.

| TODO | TASK-10 | E2E cross-dev isolation + reorder | Playwright: two devs see only their own tasks; admin reorder is reflected on next reload of dev page. | Test | TASK-07 |
